question: Is the subject recognizable as the Asian male from the reference, preserving his facial structure and short hairstyle with a fade?
Is the small scar above the subject's right eyebrow clearly visible?
Is the subject wearing a teal windbreaker that matches the color of the reference image?
Is the 'B-17' patch clearly visible, legible, and correctly positioned on the subject's left arm?
Are the secondary clothing details (black cargo pants, fingerless gloves, and dark athletic shoes) preserved?
Is the image rendered in an Art Nouveau poster style with fine black outlines and flat color blocks (no photorealism)?
Is the composition framed by a symmetrical vine and flower decorative border?
Does the image exhibit a vintage print aesthetic with subtle paper grain, fading, and sparse halftone texture?
Does the subject's mid-air leaping pose and limb proportions strictly match the structure reference line drawing?
Are the hands and feet oriented correctly according to the structure reference (e.g., hands splayed, feet angled dynamically)?
Does the background consist of simplified rooftop lines and a distant skyline, including the vertical architectural element on the right?
Is the subject positioned within the frame in the same center-left location as the structure reference?
Is the image free from obvious anatomical distortions (e.g., malformed hands, extra fingers, impossible limb bends)?
Is the line work crisp and the subject extraction clean, avoiding hard-cut edges or blurriness?
